message:
fix(cli,bd-hd89i): emit robot-mode JSON envelopes on stdout, not stderr

Pre-fix audit (CLI on 2026-04-24): the `--json` / `--robot` envelope
stream routing was inconsistent across surfaces. Sites that emitted
their structured payload via output_structured_value (diag, doctor,
status, capabilities, health, models verify, models check-update)
correctly used STDOUT, but sites whose error path bubbled up to
src/main.rs::handle_fatal_error (search, stats, sessions) emitted
the JSON envelope to STDERR.

Net effect: the basic agent pipeline `cass X --json | jq .field`
WORKED for the first group but produced EMPTY INPUT for the second,
silently. README line 80-81 documents `stdout = data only,
stderr = diagnostics, exit 0 = success` — agents reading the
README literally hit silent failures on stats/sessions/search.

Fix (option 1 from the bead — preferred): in
src/main.rs::handle_fatal_error, switch the JSON-envelope branches
(pre-formatted JSON message AND robot-mode wrapped envelope) from
eprintln! to println!. Human-readable text on the non-robot branch
still goes to stderr, preserving Unix convention. This means EVERY
robot-mode JSON envelope — data or error — now lands on stdout,
matching what the documented contract has always claimed.

Tests updated:
- stats_json_missing_db_error_envelope_matches_golden — now reads
  out.stdout
- stats_json_missing_db_error_envelope_shape_matches_golden — same
- sessions_json_missing_db_error_envelope_shape_matches_golden — same

The .golden file BYTES are unchanged (the JSON content is identical
between stderr and stdout — only the destination changed); the
tests just look at out.stdout instead of out.stderr.

Validation: rch test pass for all 3 updated tests (65s, exit=0).

Closes coding_agent_session_search-hd89i.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
